description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a prominent red hip roof made of metal panels, providing ample coverage and weather protection. The structure is supported by sturdy wooden posts and beams, with the lower half of the walls constructed from horizontal wooden planks. One long side is partially open, allowing for easy access and ventilation, while the other sides are fully enclosed with wood. The base of the posts is reinforced with concrete footings, ensuring stability. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for housing livestock in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A large, rectangular cowshed with a red hip roof, wooden plank walls, and open access on one side, supported by timber posts and concrete footings.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open side of the cowshed, revealing the wooden support posts and the spacious interior beneath the red hip roof.
description_view_1: This end view displays the fully enclosed wooden wall beneath the triangular section of the roof, with two visible support posts at the corners.
description_view_2: The rear side view highlights the long, fully enclosed wooden wall and the overhanging red roof, with the open side partially visible on the left.
description_view_3: The opposite end view shows another fully enclosed wooden wall, mirroring the other end, with the hip roof sloping down symmetrically.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank sliding door (implied, not clearly visible)
